result.stdout:
Description:
  CLI for KubeOps. Commandline tool to help with management tasks such as generating or installing CRDs.

Usage:
  KubeOps.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  g, gen, generate                Generates elements related to an operator.
  api-version, av                 Prints the actual server version of the connected kubernetes cluster.
  i, install <sln/csproj file>    Install CRDs into the cluster of the actually selected context. [default: not-found]
  u, uninstall <sln/csproj file>  Uninstall CRDs from the cluster of the actually selected context. [default: not-found]

  u, uninstall <sln/csproj file>  Uninstall CRDs from the cluster of the actually selected context. [default: not-found]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates elements related to an operator.

Usage:
  KubeOps.Cli generate [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  op, operator <name> <sln/csproj file>  Generates all required resources and configs for the operator to be built and run. [default: not-found]

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generates elements related to an operator.

Usage:
  KubeOps.Cli generate [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  op, operator <name> <sln/csproj file>  Generates all required resources and configs for the operator to be built and run. [default: not-found]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates all required resources and configs for the operator to be built and run.

Usage:
  KubeOps.Cli generate operator <name> [<sln/csproj file>] [options]

Arguments:
  <name>             Name of the operator.
  <sln/csproj file>  A solution or project file where entities are located. If omitted, the current directory is searched for a *.csproj or *.sln file. If an *.sln file is used, all projects in the solution (with the newest framework) will be searched for entities. This behaviour can be filtered by using the --project and --target-framework option. [default: not-found]

Options:
  --clear-out                                   Clear the output path before generating resources.
  --format <Json|Plain|Yaml>                    The format of the generated output. [default: Yaml]
  --out <out>                                   The path the command will write the files to. If omitted, prints output to console.
  --project <project>                           Regex pattern to filter projects in the solution to search for entities. If omitted, all projects are searched.
  --target-framework, --tfm <target-framework>  Target framework of projects in the solution to search for entities. If omitted, the newest framework is used.
  --docker-image <docker-image>                 An accessible docker image to deploy. [default: accessible-docker-image]
  --docker-image-tag <docker-image-tag>         Tag for an accessible docker image to deploy. [default: latest]
  --no-ansi                                     Disable ANSI output.
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information

If an *.sln file is used, all projects in the solution (with the newest framework) will be searched for entities. This behaviour can be filtered by using the --project and --target-framework option.   --target-framework, --tfm <target-framework>  Target framework of projects in the solution to search for entities. If omitted, the newest framework is used.
  --docker-image <docker-image>                 An accessible docker image to deploy. [default: accessible-docker-image]
  --docker-image-tag <docker-image-tag>         Tag for an accessible docker image to deploy. [default: latest]
  --no-ansi                                     Disable ANSI output.
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Install CRDs into the cluster of the actually selected context.

Usage:
  KubeOps.Cli install [<sln/csproj file>] [options]

Arguments:
  <sln/csproj file>  A solution or project file where entities are located. If omitted, the current directory is searched for a *.csproj or *.sln file. If an *.sln file is used, all projects in the solution (with the newest framework) will be searched for entities. This behaviour can be filtered by using the --project and --target-framework option. [default: not-found]

Options:
  -f, --force                                   Do not bother the user with questions and just do it.
  --project <project>                           Regex pattern to filter projects in the solution to search for entities. If omitted, all projects are searched.
  --target-framework, --tfm <target-framework>  Target framework of projects in the solution to search for entities. If omitted, the newest framework is used.
  --no-ansi                                     Disable ANSI output.
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information
